title: . Worker preference in case of bankruptcy. In the event of bankruptcy or liquidation of an
content:
. Worker preference in case of bankruptcy. In the event of bankruptcy or liquidation of an 
employer’s business, his workers shall enjoy first preference as regards their wages and other monetary 
claims, any provisions of law to the contrary notwithstanding. Such unpaid wages and monetary claims shall 
be paid in full before claims of the government and other creditors may be paid. (As amended by Section 1, 
Republic Act No. 6715, March 21, 1989)
